input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-25T00:48:05.353Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0) instead of error (exit code 8). -->
    <!-- This restores VSTest behavior for assemblies that may have no tests due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>

    <!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0) instead of error (exit code 8). -->
    <!-- This restores VSTest behavior for assemblies that may have no tests due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.reasoningText: It seems like the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` option is not being recognized or applied correctly. Let me check the log file to understand what's happening.

The log shows the command line is being passed `--minimum-expected-tests 0`, but it seems like it's dumping help text, which suggests the argument isn't being recognized properly. Let me look more carefully at the log output - the encoding seems weird (spaces between chars). Let me search for the actual error message in the log.

data.content: The `--minimum-expected-tests` option requires a **non-zero** positive integer — `0` is rejected. But the log reveals a better option: `--ignore-exit-code`. Let me use that instead:

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Treat zero discovered tests as success (exit code 0) instead of error (exit code 8). -->
    <!-- This restores VSTest behavior for assemblies that may have no tests due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found". Ignore it to restore VSTest behavior for assemblies -->
    <!-- that may produce zero tests due to conditional compilation. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-746025130a3e416ebbe572f371fac0db/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-746025130a3e416ebbe572f371fac0db/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-746025130a3e416ebbe572f371fac0db/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-746025130a3e416ebbe572f371fac0db/TestProject.csproj
@@ -1,16 +1,16 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
 
     <!-- Exit code 8 = "no tests found". Ignore it to restore VSTest behavior for assemblies -->
     <!-- that may produce zero tests due to conditional compilation. -->
     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
**The fix:** Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` via the `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` MSBuild property.

**What happened:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered, unlike VSTest which returned 0. The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag tells the runner to treat that specific exit code as success (exit 0), restoring the old behavior. Actual test failures (exit code 2) are still reported normally.
